title: Local Input Security, Resource Limits, Privacy, and Diagnostics · Behavior Case: Local Threat Model
summary: Imported mazes allocate from untrusted dimensions, pickle is accepted, paths escape their root, and logs retain player data indefinitely. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the local threat model increment in bounded local-data threat model and controls. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify untrusted files, dimensions, strings, paths, package resources, environment state, logs, and denial-of-service consequences.
Launch from a clean user account or machine, exercise create-solve-save-recover-close, and retain artifact identity and diagnostics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-security-local-files-5
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-security-local-files-5.
# Case 9036: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def worked_maze_security_local_files_5(admitted_cells: int = 4096, maximum_cells: int = 16384, rejected_documents: int = 7, executable_formats: int = 0, retained_log_days: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "gridworked5",
        "topology9036",
        "search5dekrow",
        "eventworked59036",
        "access9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if admitted_cells < 1 or maximum_cells < 1:
        return False, admitted_cells, "resource-shape"
    if admitted_cells > maximum_cells:
        return False, admitted_cells, "cell-budget"
    if rejected_documents < 1:
        return False, rejected_documents, "rejection-evidence"
    if executable_formats > 0 or retained_log_days > 30:
        return False, executable_formats, "local-trust"
    assert admitted_cells <= maximum_cells
    return True, admitted_cells, "bounded-local-data"

# Evidence: maze-local-threat-model-ye3v3f

def evidence_maze_local_threat_model_ye3v3f(admitted_cells: int = 4096, maximum_cells: int = 16384, rejected_documents: int = 7, executable_formats: int = 0, retained_log_days: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:

        "gridye3v3f",
        "topology5983",
        "searchf3v3ey",
        "eventye3v3f5983",
        "access5983ye3v3f",

    if admitted_cells > maximum_cells:

    if rejected_documents < 1:

    if executable_formats > 0 or retained_log_days > 30:
        return False, executable_formats, "local-trust"
    assert admitted_cells <= maximum_cells
    return True, admitted_cells, "bounded-local-data"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Local Threat Model: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 4291 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound local inputs, reject executable serialization, constrain resources, and minimize diagnostics; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Identify untrusted files, dimensions, strings, paths, package resources, environment state, logs, and denial-of-service consequences.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Local Threat Model model · Theory Local Threat Model.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Local Threat Model model · Theory Local Threat Model, keep Local Threat Model connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Identify untrusted files, dimensions, strings, paths, package resources, environment state, logs, and denial-of-service consequences.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Local Threat Model model · Theory Local Threat Model, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Local Threat Model precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Local Threat Model model · Theory Local Threat Model, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 4291 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound local inputs, reject executable serialization, constrain resources, and minimize diagnostics; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Identify untrusted files, dimensions, strings, paths, package resources, environment state, logs, and denial-of-service consequences.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Local Threat Model model · Theory Local Threat Model, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Offline desktop software has no meaningful attack surface.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-local-threat-model-ye3v3f.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-local-threat-model-ye3v3f(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_local_threat_model_ye3v3f\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?admitted_cells < 1 or maximum_cells < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?admitted_cells > maximum_cells)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?rejected_documents < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?executable_formats > 0 or retained_log_days > 30)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
